rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Use assign statements in implementation annexes for output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers when available to maintain traceability
Avoid direct references to AADL properties in AGREE expressions
Use helper equations for complex expressions to improve readability
Label assumptions and guarantees for better traceability
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings like assignments
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE scalar type names must be int, real, or bool
